stimulus: Before the widespread adoption of the standardized shipping container in the 1950s, dockworkers loaded ships piece by piece, packing each individual crate, barrel, or sack into the hold by hand. ______blank loading a single ship could take days or even weeks, and labor costs for shipping companies were enormous.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "Consequently" logically signals that the slow loading times and high labor costs described in this sentence were a consequence of the piece-by-piece loading method described in the previous sentence.

Choice B is incorrect because "regardless" illogically signals that the loading delays and labor costs occurred despite the method described in the previous sentence. Instead, they were a consequence of that method. Choice C is incorrect because "on the contrary" illogically signals that the information in this sentence contrasts with the previous sentence. Instead, it describes a consequence of the previous information. Choice D is incorrect because "for example" illogically signals that this sentence provides an example of the loading method. Instead, it describes a consequence of that method.
